essence: Evening priorities set as Lars first, YouTube Launch Optimizer second, Signal Studio third; a five-stage second-brain build sequence (based on Cole Medin's system, ending with KyberBot) is queued for tomorrow with clients folded into the final design; staff 4K monitor selection narrows to LG/Lenovo pending why one Samsung is suspiciously cheap.

essence: Two-track juice-shop menu locked in concept: Thai style (sweet, creamy, ice-heavy) vs natural/farang style (more fruit, less sugar) at differential pricing (~65 vs 80 baht), sold via per-fruit double-sided A4 AI-generated posters in Thai and English, with a friend recruited to taste-test and coach staff on ratios.

actions.text: Generate per-fruit bilingual A4 poster set (Thai style vs natural style) for the ~10 main fruits

essence: Juice-shop storefront plan: bamboo cascade fountain (pump ~580-1000 baht, 4-5m head to lift water 2-3m, ~20 lengths of 5-6m bamboo) plus an iced metal shelf selling bagged fruit, and a possible Friday-night mini market out front.

First payment from the new client (Lars) landed today with the remainder due over three months, easing finances; window fruit display stays minimal until regular customers exist since fruit only lasts a day, and the building will be painted.

90-day Lars engagement kickoff: David lays out the agentic-OS vision (vertical + horizontal agent stacks, brains before skills, flywheel intangibles), Lars wants tangible wins within a month and his two new machines working, and the first deliverable is an Ansible client-template playbook modeled on Mini Jan with Joe Blow placeholder params and agent-first handshake/handover documentation; weekly (initially twice-weekly) catch-ups agreed.

actions.text: Refactor Ansible playbooks to separate David-specific tools from a generic client template
Rename Mini Lars playbook to a Mac mini client template with Joe Blow placeholder username/email
Add repo description, detailed agent-first README, and flip visibility to public once private data removed
Email Lars a starter guide: install/run Ansible playbook and create ~/dev/brains second-brain structure

essence: Workflow app UI settles on three view modes — preview (designer-facing, clean), engineer (debug/prompt-template tooling), and a documented-but-future polished end-user mode needing a visual modeling language or SDK for custom layouts — with the refactor to run as background tasks plus unit tests and a Playwright-executed UAT plan; agent handshake pattern extended with lead/follower/mutual-collaborator roles.
